record.field: Tianji is an all-in-one, self-hosted observability hub that unifies website analytics, uptime monitoring, and server status in a single dashboard.

Tianji is an all-in-one, self-hosted observability hub that unifies website analytics, uptime monitoring, and server status in a single dashboard.

It consolidates what teams usually run as three separate tools (an analytics platform like Umami, an uptime monitor like Uptime Kuma, and a server-metrics collector) into one application, correlating traffic, incidents, and infrastructure health on a shared timeline. Tianji is privacy-first by design: its tracking script is lightweight and cookieless, respects Do-Not-Track, and stores minimal data, making it suitable for GDPR/CCPA-conscious deployments. It targets indie developers, small teams, and SaaS operators who want full data ownership without stitching together multiple dashboards.

Key features
  • Cookieless website and product analytics: page views, unique visitors, sessions, referrers, UTM tracking, and conversion funnels
  • Uptime monitoring with latency and error-rate tracking, plus public status pages
  • Server status monitoring (CPU, memory, disk) via a reporting agent
  • Alerting and webhook notifications when monitors or thresholds trigger
  • Surveys, telemetry, Lighthouse reports, and team collaboration
  • OpenAPI interface and an MCP server for integration and AI-agent access
Best for

Indie developers, small teams, and SaaS operators who want privacy-respecting analytics, uptime checks, and server health in one self-hosted tool, with public status pages and full control over their data.

services: tianji: image: moonrailgun/tianji ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:12345' environment: DATABASE_URL: postgresql://tianji:tianji@postgres:5432/tianji JWT_SECRET: {{ jwt_secret }} ALLOW_REGISTER: 'false' ALLOW_OPENAPI: 'true' depends_on: - postgres container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgres: image: postgres:15.4-alpine environment: POSTGRES_DB: tianji POSTGRES_USER: tianji POSTGRES_PASSWORD: tianji volumes: - ./tianji_db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
